messages.content:
Hook: Catchy sentence or sentences
that draw reader’s attention
a. Should be 1-2 sentences
b. Can Be:
i. A Quote
ii. A Question
iii. A Bold Statement
2. Background Information: Basic
information about your topic
a. Should be 2-3 sentences
b. Assumes your reader is unaware of
the topic
c. Don’t give too much information;
save that for body paragraphs
3. Thesis Statement: The main
idea/argument of your essay
a. Should be 1-2 sentences
b. Needs to be able to be argued
c. Explains your opinion of the topic
d. Describes how you will be arguing
that point
The legal age to buy and consume
tobacco should be lowered.

Hook: "Did you know that the legal age to buy and consume tobacco could be changing?"
Background Information: Many countries and states have set the legal age to purchase and consume tobacco at 18 or 21 years old. However, there is an ongoing debate about whether this age limit should be lowered.
Thesis Statement: It is my belief that the legal age to buy and consume tobacco should be lowered to [insert age], as there are several compelling arguments that support this change. In this essay, I will explore the potential benefits and address the counterarguments surrounding this controversial topic.

Read this sentence.
Although people in New York City are accustomed to buying bottled water, taste tests show that Manhattans tap water actually tastes better than a specific brand of bottled water that costs 55 dollars.
How should the underlined word be revised?
A. Leave as is.
B. Manhattan's
C. Manhattans's
D. Manhattans'
A. Leave as is.

Which sentence has an apostrophe error?

A. A person with this condition can't enjoy music.
B. Amusics hear and perceive sounds, but they don't perceive differences in pitch.
C. There are people who arent able to sing, nor are they able to perceive music.
D. Amusia isn't a defect in how the ears function.
C. There are people who arent able to sing, nor are they able to perceive music.

Which sentence uses apostrophes incorrectly?

A. A war is rising between France's pastry bakers and bakery owners about the use of pre-made dough in croissants.
B. Lawmakers must decide if it is the bakery owner's obligation to warn shoppers that croissants are made from pre-made dough.
C. Some people say you can tell a real croissant by it's taste.
D. The croissant's history in France possibly dates back to the arrival of Marie Antoinette.
C. Some people say you can tell a real croissant by it's taste.

Read these sentences.
Some flowers continue to grow and bloom for several seasons. These are called perennials.
Which best combines the sentences?

A. Flowers, which continue to grow and bloom for several seasons, are called perennials.
B. Perennials, which are flowers, continue to grow and bloom for several seasons.
C. Flowers that continue to grow and bloom for several seasons are called perennials.
D. Perennials continue to grow and bloom for several seasons, and they are flowers.
A. Flowers, which continue to grow and bloom for several seasons, are called perennials.

Which version of the sentence uses correct punctuation?

A. The band, that, wins the contest will get to play at the festival.
B. The band that wins the contest, will get to play at the festival.
C. The band that wins the contest will get to play at the festival.
D. The band, that wins the contest, will get to play at the festival.
C. The band that wins the contest will get to play at the festival.

Which sentence includes an elliptical clause?

A. I try to feed her and play with her, but she ignores me.
B. The whims and tempers of cats can be mysterious to their owners.
C. The family cat has always liked my brother more than me.
D. Some friendly cats can be just as affectionate and warm as dogs are
A. I try to feed her and play with her, but she ignores me.

In which sentence are the underlined words a restrictive clause?

A. They plan to travel in the summer and settle down in the winter.
B. Our grandparents are building a houseboat to live in after they retire.
C. Their dream has always been to live close to the ocean.
D. The design that they are following is simple and modest.
D. The design that they are following is simple and modest.

Read this sentence.
Some animals feed on acorns, and these animals actually help plant trees by spreading seeds around.
Which is the best revision of the sentence?

A. Animals that feed on acorns actually help plant trees by spreading seeds around.
B. Actually they help plant trees by spreading seeds around when animals feed on acorns.
C. By spreading seeds around there are animals that feed on acorns and actually help plant trees.
D. Animals feed on acorns, and they actually help plant trees by spreading seeds around.
A. Animals that feed on acorns actually help plant trees by spreading seeds around.

Which version of the sentence uses correct punctuation?

A. Plants that have long roots are often, more resistant to drought.
B. Plants that have long roots, are often more resistant to drought.
C. Plants that have long roots are often more resistant to drought.
D. Plants, that have long roots, are often more resistant to drought
C. Plants that have long roots are often more resistant to drought.

Which sentence option correctly uses a semicolon?

A. Mark travels a lot with his family; his favorite place to visit is Ecuador.
B. Mark travels a lot with his family his favorite place to visit; is Ecuador.
C. Mark travels a lot with his family his favorite place to visit is; Ecuador.
D. Mark travels a lot; with his family his favorite place to visit is Ecuador.
A. Mark travels a lot with his family; his favorite place to visit is Ecuador.

Which sentence option is punctuated correctly?

A. As you heard in her election speech Samantha has some very progressive ideas, for rewarding student involvement in community activities.
B. As you heard in her election speech, Samantha has some very progressive ideas for rewarding student involvement in community activities.
C. As you heard, in her election speech, Samantha has some very progressive ideas for rewarding student involvement in community activities.
D. As you heard in her election speech Samantha has some very, progressive ideas for rewarding student involvement in community activities
B. As you heard in her election speech, Samantha has some very progressive ideas for rewarding student involvement in community activities.

Read this sentence.
When training a dog, the trainer must remember two things the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language, and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind.
Which version of the sentence shows the correct use of the colon?

A. When training a dog: the trainer must remember two things the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language, and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind.
B. When training a dog, the trainer must remember: two things the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language, and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind..
C. When training a dog, the trainer must remember two things the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language: and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind.
D. When training a dog, the trainer must remember two things: the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language, and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind.
D. When training a dog, the trainer must remember two things: the dog does not comprehend the trainer's language, and the dog cannot read the trainer's mind.

Which sentence is punctuated correctly?

A. Keats said that truth is beauty; Tolstoy said that the idea that beauty is good is an illusion.
B. Some believe that; beauty indicates natural goodness others believe that beauty is merely superficial.
C. Beauty may be in the eye of the beholder writers have different ideas; about what beauty means.
D. Even the concept of beauty may be disputed people often fail to see beauty; that is obvious to others.
A. Keats said that truth is beauty; Tolstoy said that the idea that beauty is good is an illusion.

Read this sentence.
The harp is a beautiful instrument though very difficult to master.
Which version of the above sentence is punctuated correctly?

A. The harp, is a beautiful instrument, though very difficult to master.
B. The harp is a beautiful instrument, though very, difficult to master.
C. The harp is a beautiful instrument, though very difficult, to master.
D. The harp is a beautiful instrument, though very difficult to master.
D. The harp is a beautiful instrument, though very difficult to master.

Read this sentence:
When the weather cleared they traveled up the coast in the new sailboat.
Which version of the above sentence is punctuated correctly?

A. When the weather cleared, they traveled, up the coast in the new sailboat.
B. When the weather cleared they traveled, up the coast, in the new sailboat.
C. When the weather cleared they traveled up the coast, in the new sailboat.
D> When the weather cleared, they traveled up the coast in the new sailboat
D. When the weather cleared, they traveled up the coast in the new sailboat.

Which sentence is punctuated correctly?

A. The growing use of tractors, which made farm work more efficient cut down the demand for draft horses.
B. Draft horses, which have been bred to pull heavy loads, often weigh more than a ton.
C. Sampson who was a 19th-century Shire horse, still holds the record for the largest horse ever.
D. The Shire horse, which, was once a common working breed is now considered rare.
B. Draft horses, which have been bred to pull heavy loads, often weigh more than a ton.

Which sentence uses correct punctuation?

A. While children, shrieked and raced around the park their parents stood in groups and talked.
B. As the ball soared across the field a dog, sprung up to snatch it out of the air.
C. Because of its elaborate playground, the park was a popular setting for parties and picnics.
D. Swinging, gleefully on the monkey bars the girl called her friends to join her.
C. Because of its elaborate playground, the park was a popular setting for parties and picnics.
